tweets.text: Together with @AOC, I'm introducing the AI Data Center Moratorium Act — calling for a pause on new AI data center construction and frontier AI development until we have proper safety guardrails in place.

LLMs fail at physical world understanding in ways that matter for robotics and embodied AI — worse than a cat at anticipating consequences. The path forward is world models (JEPA), not bigger generative token predictors.
Current vision systems in robotics use ConvNets and CLIP-like embeddings, not LLMs. The architecture that will win in embodied AI is not based on next-token prediction.
The benchmark obsession in AI is a category error. Judging AGI by human-test mimicry tells you nothing about whether the system can handle novel physical situations it was never trained on.

JEPA and world models aren't just academic alternatives — they're the only architectures that can reason about physical causality without being told explicitly. That's what you need for real autonomous agents.

Auto-review is now live in Codex. A guardian agent evaluates the safety of proposed actions, cutting down human approvals to only the essential cases. Big step for making agentic coding practical in production.
